title: paraprasophyllum suttonii
side_effects: Paraprasophyllum suttonii can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, or respiratory issues. Ingesting the plant's toxins can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure may lead to headaches and fatigue. If the plant's sap comes into contact with the eyes, it can cause redness, itching, and temporary blindness. Improper use can also increase the risk of kidney or liver damage, making it essential to handle the plant with caution and avoid ingesting it altogether.
